text:
I caught myself one day ordering tests because a textbook told me to.

Not because I had thought it through. Not because I had looked at the patient and the numbers and the story and come to a decision. Because the pathway said: next step, order this.

And I was following the pathway like a checklist. Not like a physician.

Caleb never worked from a checklist. He worked from understanding. He knew why every step existed and so he could adapt when the problem did not follow the script.

I was not doing that. I was executing instructions I did not fully own.

That scared me more than the freezing did. Because at least when I froze I knew something was wrong. This felt like competence and it was not.
